methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Valar Atomics is a Series G nuclear energy company founded by Isaiah P. Taylor that has raised $149M to date. The sector includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 notable private competitors (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, ARC Clean Technology), though none provide sufficient comparability at the same stage or valuation tier for direct peer median analysis.

The valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement to a $6.0B baseline derived from total capital raised. This yields an implied valuation of $7.5B. The nuclear energy peer set, while populated with four same-sector companies, lacks the depth needed for robust peer-median triangulation; applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the sector's specialized risk and growth profile.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.4B to $8.6B, with a median point of $7.5B. This range reflects ±15% dispersion around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for frontier nuclear technology at late stages, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and timeline to revenue or regulatory milestones. Given the sparse comparable set, the reliability of this estimate hinges on the representativeness of the $6.0B baseline and the applicability of standard venture stage mechanics to emerging nuclear companies.
